## Overview

**v0.4.0 — cross-platform unification + Windows IME parity with macOS.**

This release pulls the Windows and macOS code paths together: tab bar
layout, tab actions, close policy, and IME pre-edit math now live in
shared `tab_layout.zig` / `tab_actions.zig` modules with a `Host`
interface, so a single fix lands on both platforms instead of two.
Windows gains the inline IME pre-edit overlay (purple, mac-equivalent),
candidate-window cursor tracking for Japanese / Chinese / Korean Hanja
conversion, and native textbox UX for tab rename. About ~400 lines of
duplicated cross-platform code were eliminated along the way.

## Downloads

| Platform | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Windows | `tildaz-v0.4.0-windows.zip` | unzip anywhere. Includes `tildaz.exe` + bundled ConPTY runtime. |
| macOS (universal) | `tildaz-v0.4.0-macos.dmg` | Apple Silicon + Intel single binary. Drag `TildaZ.app` into Applications. macOS 13 Ventura or later. |

First-launch on macOS: the app is ad-hoc signed, so macOS may block the
first open. Right-click `TildaZ.app` → Open, or run
`xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /Applications/TildaZ.app`. Then grant
Accessibility + Input Monitoring permissions once.

## What's new since v0.3.3

### Windows — IME pre-edit overlay (parity with macOS)

- **Inline pre-edit on the cursor**
  ([#164](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/164)). Windows used to
  show the system default IME composition popup (small floating window
  away from the cursor) for Korean, Japanese, and Chinese input. tildaz
  now intercepts `WM_IME_STARTCOMPOSITION` / `WM_IME_COMPOSITION`
  (`GCS_COMPSTR`) / `WM_IME_ENDCOMPOSITION`, reads the composition
  string with `ImmGetCompositionStringW`, and draws the pre-edit text
  inline at the cursor with the same purple background tildaz on macOS
  uses. Both terminal cell input and tab-rename input get the overlay.
- **Hanja / kanji / hanzi candidate popup follows the cursor**
  (#164 1d). `ImmSetCompositionWindow(CFS_POINT, cursor_pixel)` is
  called every frame so the OS-drawn candidate list (Japanese
  conversion, Pinyin selection, Korean Hanja conversion) appears
  next to the cursor instead of the screen origin / last-known
  position. Korean's commit-on-keypress flow is unaffected (no
  candidate popup needed); Japanese / Chinese / Hanja get a
  noticeable UX upgrade.
- **Native textbox UX for tab rename**:
  - Click into the renaming tab's text → cursor moves to the click
    position, IME pre-edit is committed (so the in-progress jamo /
    kana lands in the buffer where it was before the click) and
    cancelled. No more "click somewhere to fix a typo and the
    pre-edit follows my cursor" wart.
  - Mid-string typing pushes only the characters after the cursor
    rightward by the pre-edit's width, mirroring what every standard
    text field does. Commit drops the pre-edit cells in place;
    Escape returns the trailing characters back to where they were.
  - Cursor-follow scroll reserves a fixed 2-cell slot at the right
    edge for the pre-edit, regardless of whether composition is
    active. Eliminates the visible cursor jump that used to happen
    every time a Korean syllable committed.

### Cross-platform unification

- **`tab_layout.zig` — pure layout / hit-test module**
  ([#159](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/159) Phase 1).
  `<` / `>` / `+` button placement, viewport scroll math, tab hit
  test, and the rename text-hit byte mapping live in one module
  with no platform dependencies. Both hosts call the same
  functions; both renderers consume the same `Layout` struct.
- **`tab_actions.zig` + `Host` interface** (#159 Phase 2-3).
  `switchTab` / `nextTab` / `prevTab` / `resetActive` /
  `closeActive` / `closeByPtr` / `closeIndex` /
  `copyActiveSelection` / `routePaste` / `checkAtLimitAndDialog`
  are cross-platform helpers. Each platform host fills a small
  `Host` struct with five callbacks (`invalidate`, `rename_active`,
  `insert_rename_cp`, `clipboard_copy`, `terminate`); the helpers
  do the post-action sequencing (override clear → invalidate →
  optional terminate) so call sites are one line. Last-tab-closed
  is now consistent: both platforms call `host.terminate()` from
  the same path whether the tab closed via Cmd+W / Ctrl+Shift+W,
  click of the X button, or a shell-level `exit`.
- **Tab interaction structs unified**
  ([#163](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/163) Phase 4).
  `tab_layout.Layout`, `tab_interaction.RenameView`, and
  `tab_interaction.DragView` are now the single representation
  consumed by both renderers. Tab titles are passed as
  `[]const []const u8` slices on both sides (the Windows-only
  `TabTitle{ptr, len}` struct is gone). The macOS-only IME
  pre-edit is split out as a separate `tab_rename_preedit`
  argument; everything else matches.
- **IME pre-edit math unified** (#163 option A). The
  `cursorReserve(cw)` / `computeAdvanceTotal(text, cw)` /
  `cursorScrollOffset(title, cursor_byte, cw, max_w,
  preedit_advance)` helpers in `tab_layout.zig` are called by both
  renderers and by both hosts' click → cursor logic. The
  cursor-follow-scroll algorithm (probe to the cursor + reserve
  the pre-edit width on the right so the cursor + pre-edit end
  pin to the right edge) is now identical on both platforms, and
  a fix lands in one place.

### Tab bar polish

- **Firefox-pattern tab scroll, exact alignment** (#159 follow-ups).
  Pressing `<` aligns the leftmost visible tab's left edge to the
  arrow; pressing `>` aligns the rightmost visible tab's right
  edge to the arrow. The "gap" concept between tabs and arrows is
  gone; tabs sit directly next to `<` / `+` / `>`. Floor / ceil
  arithmetic ensures no off-by-one at boundaries.
- **Arrow / `+` button width 36 → 24 pt**. The buttons were
  noticeably wider than tall (28 pt) and read as horizontal
  rectangles. 24 pt is narrower than the 28 pt bar height, giving
  the chevrons a slight vertical-portrait feel without losing
  click target size.
- **32-tab limit + "Tab limit reached" dialog**. Both platforms
  cap the session at 32 tabs (`session_core.MAX_TABS`). The `+`
  button hides automatically when the limit is reached and
  reappears when a tab is closed. Pressing Cmd+T / Ctrl+Shift+T
  while at the limit shows a dialog so the user gets feedback
  even when the visual cue (no `+` button) is easy to miss.
- **Glyph guard fix**. `<` / `>` / `+` glyphs are no longer drawn
  when their layout box has zero width — the off-screen `+`
  glyph that used to peek out at 32 tabs is gone.

### Native rename UX

- **Click in the rename text → cursor moves, no commit** (#164
  follow-up). Previously every click anywhere — including inside
  the renaming tab's own text — would commit and end the rename.
  Now clicks inside the same tab's text area move the cursor to
  the click position; clicks outside (other tabs, the close
  button, the terminal, the arrows) still commit as before.
- **Pre-edit at mid-cursor pushes the right side, not the cursor**
  (#164 1c-fix2). Typing Korean / Chinese / Japanese in the middle
  of an existing tab name now temporarily shifts only the
  characters after the cursor by the pre-edit's width. Commit
  inserts the IME's output at the cursor position; Escape /
  cancel returns the trailing text to where it was.
- **Pre-edit consistency at fast typing speeds** (#164 1c-fix5).
  The cursor-follow-scroll reserve is fixed at 2 cells regardless
  of whether composition is active, so there's no per-keystroke
  jitter when a Korean syllable transitions from "composing" to
  "committed". The visible position of the cursor is rock-stable
  while typing.

## Multilingual input (clarification)

To make the scope of #166 below clear: **Korean, Japanese, Chinese,
Vietnamese, and Indic input all work end-to-end on both Windows and
macOS**. The IME composition path (typing → pre-edit → candidate
popup → commit) is fully supported:

- The OS IME draws its own candidate-list popup for *kanji / hanzi /
  Hanja during composition*. We don't draw it ourselves; we only
  position it next to the cursor on Windows via
  `ImmSetCompositionWindow(CFS_POINT, …)`.
- Our purple inline overlay shows the in-progress jamo / hiragana /
  pinyin while the IME composes.
- On commit, the final glyph cluster lands in the PTY (or rename
  buffer) through the standard `WM_CHAR` / `insertText:` path.

#166 below is about *committed text → Hanja* **reconversion** —
i.e. you've already typed `한국`, and you want to convert it
afterwards via Option+Return. That's a separate post-commit feature
and is independent of typing-time input.

## Known issues

Same as v0.3.3 — see [`v0.3.3.md`](v0.3.3.md). One known limitation
explicitly chosen for this release:

- **macOS Hanja / kanji *reconversion* (Option+Return on
  already-committed text)**
  ([#166](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/166)). Apple's
  Korean / Japanese / Chinese IMEs let the user pick a previously
  committed run and convert it to Hanja / kanji / hanzi via
  `Option+Return`. This requires the view to implement the
  `NSTextInputClient` *reconversion* surface
  (`attributedSubstring(forProposedRange:actualRange:)`,
  `firstRect(forCharacterRange:actualRange:)`, etc.). Apple's own
  Terminal.app does not implement these, so reconversion does not
  work there either — only `NSTextView`-based apps like TextEdit do.
  tildaz on macOS follows the same platform default. Composition-
  path Hanja (converting *while still typing*, before commit) is
  unaffected and works normally; only post-commit reconversion is
  missing. Tracked as enhancement #166.

## Verification

- [ ] Windows: `tildaz-v0.4.0-windows.zip` extracts; `tildaz.exe`
      launches; F1 toggles; emoji showcase line renders.
- [ ] Windows: with Korean / Japanese / Chinese IME active, type
      into a terminal cell — the in-progress syllable shows
      inline at the cursor on a purple background, *not* in a
      separate IME composition window. With Japanese / Chinese
      IME, the kanji / hanzi candidate popup appears next to
      the cursor.
- [ ] Windows: double-click a tab → tab name becomes editable.
      Type Korean characters; mid-string click moves the cursor
      to the click position; the syllable in progress lands
      in-place. Escape cancels the rename. Commit (Enter) keeps
      the typed name.
- [ ] Windows: opening 32 tabs — the `+` button disappears.
      Pressing Ctrl+Shift+T at 32 tabs shows the "Tab limit
      reached" dialog. Closing one tab brings `+` back.
- [ ] macOS: same tab-rename click → cursor behavior; same
      mid-cursor pre-edit push-right; same 32-tab limit + dialog.
- [ ] Both platforms: click `<` / `>` arrows → leftmost / rightmost
      visible tab aligns flush to the arrow's inner edge. No
      partial-tab peek-through into the arrow area.
- [ ] Both platforms: shell `exit` from the last tab → app quits.
      Same path used by closing the last tab via Cmd+W / Ctrl+Shift+W
      and by clicking the last tab's `×`.
